title: Sequential Dependency Timeout Invalidates Execution
scenario: A clinical action depends on a prior step that must be followed within a fixed interval, but the dependent action is attempted after that interval.
initial_state: The prerequisite step was completed and temporarily supported the dependent action.
state_transition: The permissible interval between the prerequisite step and dependent execution elapsed.
execution_boundary: The dependent clinical action is evaluated before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Temporal admissibility includes continuity between ordered clinical steps when sequence timing is execution-critical.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because the dependent action no longer falls within the valid sequence interval.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when a sequential timing dependency expires.
clinical_action: Execute dependent clinical action after sequence timeout
